2604.09024.title: Leave My Images Alone: Preventing Multi-Modal Large Language Models from Analyzing Images via Visual Prompt Injection
2604.09024.paper_summary: Multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) have emerged as powerful tools for analyzing Internet-scale image data, offering significant benefits but also raising critical safety and societal concerns. In particular, open-weight MLLMs may be misused to extract sensitive information from personal images at scale, such as identities, locations, or other private details. In this work, we propose ImageProtector, a user-side method that proactively protects images before sharing by embedding a carefully crafted, nearly imperceptible perturbation that acts as a visual prompt injection attack on MLLMs. As a result, when an adversary analyzes a protected image with an MLLM, the MLLM is consistently induced to generate a refusal response such as "I'm sorry, I can't help with that request." We empirically demonstrate the effectiveness of ImageProtector across six MLLMs and four datasets. Additionally, we evaluate three potential countermeasures, Gaussian noise, DiffPure, and adversarial training, and show that while they partially mitigate the impact of ImageProtector, they simultaneously degrade model accuracy and/or efficiency. Our study focuses on the practically important setting of open-weight MLLMs and large-scale automated image analysis, and highlights both the promise and the limitations of perturbation-based privacy protection.
2604.09024.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）已成為分析互聯網規模圖像數據的強大工具，提供了顯著的好處，但也引發了關鍵的安全和社會問題。特別是，開放權重的MLLMs可能被濫用來大規模提取個人圖像中的敏感信息，例如身份、位置或其他私人細節。在這項工作中，我們提出了ImageProtector，一種用戶端方法，通過嵌入精心設計的、幾乎不可察覺的擾動，在分享之前主動保護圖像，該擾動作為對MLLMs的視覺提示注入攻擊。因此，當對手使用MLLM分析受保護的圖像時，該MLLM會持續被引導生成拒絕響應，例如「抱歉，我無法幫助滿足該請求。」我們在六個MLLM和四個數據集上實證展示了ImageProtector的有效性。此外，我們評估了三種潛在的對策，高斯噪聲、DiffPure和對抗訓練，並顯示雖然它們部分減輕了ImageProtector的影響，但同時降低了模型的準確性和/或效率。我們的研究專注於開放權重MLLM和大規模自動圖像分析的實際重要設置，並突顯了基於擾動的隱私保護的前景和局限性。
2604.09024.author: Zedian Shao et.al.
2604.09024.authors: Zedian Shao, Hongbin Liu, Yuepeng Hu, Neil Zhenqiang Gong
